who.subtitle: W h o   W e   A r e
who.title: Who We Are
who.description: Established in 1985, Abdout was founded with the goal of supporting and developing the local community by providing exceptional customs clearance and logistics services that enhance trade efficiency and meet business needs transparently. Since our establishment, we have been committed to building a service system based on reliability, expertise, and sustainable business practices.

why.subtitle: W h y   U s
why.title: Why Us
why.description: We believe that efficient logistics is the foundation for successful trade. With our dedication to offering high-quality services that reflect the aspirations of businesses, we meet growing needs efficiently. Our commitment to transparency, professional values, and sustainable practices makes us the trusted choice for customs clearance services in Port Sudan.

mission.subtitle: O u r   M i s s i o n
mission.title: Our Vision and Mission
mission.description: To provide an effective logistics environment that supports businesses, offers reliable services, and launches developmental initiatives and strategic partnerships that enhance trade efficiency and economic growth. We strive to be the leading customs clearance company that promotes seamless international trade.

goals.title: Business Support
goals.description: Building strong business relationships through reliable services that support importers and exporters in achieving their goals.

goals.title: Quality Services
goals.description: Delivering reliable, high-quality customs clearance and logistics services that meet the evolving needs of our clients with transparency and efficiency.

goals.title: Sustainable Growth
goals.description: Implementing sustainable business practices that ensure long-term growth and prosperity for our company and clients.

testimonial.quote: Abdout transformed how we handle shipments. Real-time tracking and automated alerts have made our operations incredibly smooth and efficient.
testimonial.author: Ahmed Hassan

sudan-customs-digital-system.title: Sudan Customs Goes Digital
sudan-customs-digital-system.description: Sudan Customs Authority signed a landmark agreement with Substansible for an Advanced Cargo Declaration System. Director General Lt. Gen. Salah Ahmed Ibrahim confirmed the system enables pre-loading cargo verification and prevents non-compliant goods from entering.
sudan-customs-digital-system.content: Sudan Customs Authority signed a landmark agreement with Substansible for an Advanced Cargo Declaration System. Director General Lt. Gen. Salah Ahmed Ibrahim confirmed the system enables pre-loading cargo verification and prevents non-compliant goods from entering. This digital integration aligns with international trade standards and marks a significant step toward modernizing Sudan's customs infrastructure.

red-sea-shipping-crisis.title: Red Sea Shipping Crisis Impact
red-sea-shipping-crisis.description: Houthi attacks have disrupted 10% of global seaborne trade, forcing ships to reroute around Africa. Aid shipments to Port Sudan now take months instead of weeks. Despite a May 2025 ceasefire, attacks resumed in July.
red-sea-shipping-crisis.content: Houthi attacks have disrupted 10% of global seaborne trade, forcing ships to reroute around Africa. Aid shipments to Port Sudan now take months instead of weeks. Despite a May 2025 ceasefire, attacks resumed in July. Maersk completed its first test transit in December 2025, signaling cautious optimism for the future of Red Sea shipping lanes.

port-sudan-operations-update.title: Port Sudan Operations Update
port-sudan-operations-update.description: Despite ongoing conflict, Port Sudan maintains operations. Container terminal resumed service in May 2025, and bulk cargo including wheat arrives regularly. Bank closures create documentation challenges.
port-sudan-operations-update.content: Despite ongoing conflict, Port Sudan maintains operations. Container terminal resumed service in May 2025, and bulk cargo including wheat arrives regularly. Bank closures create documentation challenges, but workarounds keep trade flowing through this critical Red Sea gateway. The port remains a vital lifeline for humanitarian aid and commercial goods.

africa-single-window-revolution.title: Africa's Single Window Revolution
africa-single-window-revolution.description: African nations are modernizing customs through ASYCUDA technology. The Gambia saw 23% revenue increase after implementation. Cloud-based systems connect government agencies on a single platform.
africa-single-window-revolution.content: African nations are modernizing customs through ASYCUDA technology. The Gambia saw 23% revenue increase after implementation. Cloud-based systems connect government agencies on a single platform, reducing clearance times and costs for traders across the continent. This digital transformation is reshaping how Africa trades.

customs-automation-trends-2025.title: 2025 Customs Automation Trends
customs-automation-trends-2025.description: AI-powered document verification and automated customs declarations are transforming border processing. Green corridors for trusted traders reduce wait times significantly.
customs-automation-trends-2025.content: AI-powered document verification and automated customs declarations are transforming border processing. Green corridors for trusted traders reduce wait times. Digital customs integration enables data-driven decisions and ensures secure, transparent trade systems. These technologies are setting new standards for international trade.

sudan-pre-shipment-verification-guide.title: Pre-Shipment Verification Guide
sudan-pre-shipment-verification-guide.description: Sudan's SSMO requires a Certificate of Inspection (CoI) for regulated products. Working with TUV Rheinland, exporters can ensure compliance with national and international standards.
sudan-pre-shipment-verification-guide.content: Sudan's SSMO requires a Certificate of Inspection (CoI) for regulated products. Working with TUV Rheinland, exporters can ensure compliance with national and international standards before shipment, facilitating smooth customs clearance at Sudanese ports. This guide covers the essential steps for successful verification.

sudan-afcfta-path-forward.title: Sudan & AfCFTA: The Path Forward
sudan-afcfta-path-forward.description: Sudan remains a non-state party to the African Continental Free Trade Area. Ratification would open preferential trade access to 54 African nations.
sudan-afcfta-path-forward.content: Sudan remains a non-state party to the African Continental Free Trade Area. Ratification would open preferential trade access to 54 African nations. As 11 countries now actively trade under AfCFTA rules, pressure grows for non-members to join. The potential benefits for Sudan's economy could be substantial.

gulf-ports-technology-innovation.title: Gulf Ports Lead Technology Innovation
gulf-ports-technology-innovation.description: GCC ports are investing $110 billion by 2030 in smart logistics. Jebel Ali sets cargo records while Hamad Port leads in automation.
gulf-ports-technology-innovation.content: GCC ports are investing $110 billion by 2030 in smart logistics. Jebel Ali sets cargo records while Hamad Port leads in automation. Fujairah's new MarHub platform integrates vessel calls, security, and analytics. The region is becoming a global maritime tech leader, setting examples for ports worldwide.

smart-ports-global-trade.title: Smart Ports Shaping Global Trade
smart-ports-global-trade.description: IoT sensors, AI scheduling, and blockchain tracking are revolutionizing port operations. Automated container handling and real-time cargo visibility help ports manage disruptions.
smart-ports-global-trade.content: IoT sensors, AI scheduling, and blockchain tracking are revolutionizing port operations. Automated container handling and real-time cargo visibility help ports manage disruptions. These technologies offer a roadmap for emerging market ports seeking efficiency gains and competitive advantages in global trade.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Your guide to our services and plans.

q1.question: What's included in the One Project plan?
q1.answer: The One Project plan includes complete custom development for a single automation solution, including consultation, development, testing, and deployment.

q2.question: How does the hourly billing work for Strategic Partner?
q2.answer: With Strategic Partner, you get access to our development team at $10/hour. Perfect for ongoing projects, maintenance, or when you need flexible development resources.

q3.question: Can I switch between plans?
q3.answer: Yes, you can upgrade or downgrade your plan at any time. Changes will be reflected in your next billing cycle.

q4.question: Do you offer enterprise solutions?
q4.answer: Absolutely! For enterprise needs, we offer custom solutions with dedicated teams, SLAs, and specialized support. Contact us for a tailored quote.

q5.question: What support is included?
q5.answer: All plans include email support. Higher tiers get priority response times and dedicated account managers for seamless communication.

process.title: Our Clearance Process
process.subtitle: A streamlined 7-step process ensuring smooth customs clearance.

steps.title: Document Submission
steps.description: Submit your shipping documents through our digital platform or in person.

steps.title: Document Verification
steps.description: Our experts verify all documents for compliance and accuracy.

steps.title: Customs Declaration
steps.description: We prepare and file your customs declaration with authorities.

steps.title: Duty Assessment
steps.description: Calculation of applicable duties, taxes, and fees.

steps.title: Payment Processing
steps.description: Secure payment handling for all customs charges.

steps.title: Inspection
steps.description: Coordination with authorities for any required inspections.

steps.title: Release & Delivery
steps.description: Cargo release and arrangement of final delivery.

cta.title: Ready to Ship?
cta.subtitle: Get started with your customs clearance today.

sections.readyBody: Talk to our team and move your shipment through Port Sudan with confidence.

description.firstLine: Licensed Port Sudan brokers handling your entire
description.secondLine: customs declaration from filing to release.

customs-clearance.detailedDescription: End-to-end customs clearance at Port Sudan. Licensed brokers classify your goods, compute duties, file declarations, secure release orders, and support post-clearance audits. Works across sea, air, and land cargo.
features: HS code classification and tariff determination
Declaration filing with Sudan Customs authority
Sea, air, and land freight coverage
advantagesDescriptions: Certified agents with direct access to customs officers at every terminal.
Standard declarations released within 24 hours once duty is paid.
No hidden charges. Fee schedule agreed before filing.
Deep operational knowledge of Port Sudan's inspection and release workflows.
Liability cover on every shipment we clear on your behalf.

process.title: Document intake
process.body: Share commercial invoice, packing list, and B/L. We verify completeness before filing.

process.title: HS classification
process.body: We classify line items against the Sudan customs tariff book.

process.title: Duty computation
process.body: We compute customs duty, VAT, excise, and development fee on CIF basis.

process.title: Declaration filing
process.body: We file the declaration electronically with Sudan Customs.

process.title: Duty payment
process.body: You pay duty directly or through us. We receipt and reconcile.

process.title: Release and gate-pass
process.body: We secure the release order and coordinate gate-pass for collection.

description.secondLine: validation, and legalization for Sudan imports.

document-processing.detailedDescription: We prepare and validate every document Sudan Customs requires. Commercial invoices, packing lists, certificates of origin, insurance certificates, and consular legalization. Errors caught before they cost you demurrage.
features: Bill of Lading and Air Waybill processing
advantages: Zero rejection rate on filed documents
Direct coordination with banks and embassies
Archive of all filings for 7 years
advantagesDescriptions: Documents pre-validated against the Sudan customs checklist so they never bounce.
We liaise directly with correspondent banks and the consulate for attestations.
Typos or missing fields amended and re-issued within the same working day.
Compliant electronic archive available for audit anytime in the next seven years.
Drafts issued in both Arabic and English to satisfy every stakeholder.

process.title: Document checklist
process.body: We hand you the exact checklist for your cargo type.

process.title: Draft review
process.body: We review supplier drafts before you commit to shipment.

process.title: Amendments
process.body: We coordinate corrections with the supplier if errors appear.

process.title: Legalization
process.body: We walk papers through consulate and chamber as needed.

process.title: Customs submission
process.body: We attach all documents to the customs declaration.

process.title: Archive
process.body: We archive scanned copies to your dashboard for future audits.

inspection-coordination.detailedDescription: We manage the full inspection leg for regulated products. Food, chemicals, construction materials, vehicles, textiles, and electronics all require SSMO Certificate of Inspection. We coordinate sampling, chase results, and handle appeals when tests fail.
advantagesDescriptions: We advise on inspection requirements before the goods leave the origin port.
Long-standing relationships shorten scheduling and prevent duplicate sampling.
Partner labs return standard results within 48 to 72 hours.
Documented track record of overturning incorrect rejection decisions.
We assess compliance risk before you commit to the shipment.

process.title: Compliance review
process.body: We check whether your goods need SSMO CoI before shipping.

process.title: Sampling coordination
process.body: We arrange SSMO inspector attendance at the terminal.

process.title: Lab submission
process.body: Samples are delivered to accredited laboratories.

process.title: Results tracking
process.body: We chase results daily and forward them to you.

process.title: Certificate issuance
process.body: The CoI is issued and attached to the customs file.

process.title: Appeal on failure
process.body: We prepare and submit appeals for adverse results.

description.secondLine: port storage and terminal handling charges.

port-fees-demurrage.detailedDescription: Port fees and demurrage eat margins silently. We track free time, calculate charges before they accrue, negotiate extensions, and dispute incorrect invoices. Our dashboard shows live demurrage exposure across your fleet.
features: Live demurrage calculator with free-time tracking
Free-time extension requests to shipping lines
advantages: Direct WhatsApp notification on critical dates
Escalation to shipping line head office
advantagesDescriptions: Tiered alerts give you time to act before demurrage starts accruing.
Our negotiation track record saves about 30 percent on extension requests.
Critical-day notices land on your phone when demurrage is about to hit.
Every charge is matched to a tariff line and challenged when it does not fit.
When the terminal refuses, we escalate the case to the line's head office.

process.title: Arrival notice
process.body: We log arrival date and free-time window from the B/L.

process.title: Daily monitoring
process.body: The dashboard tracks days remaining on every container.

process.title: Proactive alerts
process.body: Alerts fire at 7, 3, and 1 days before demurrage starts.

process.title: Extension request
process.body: We file extension requests with the shipping line when needed.

process.title: Invoice review
process.body: Every port invoice is reconciled against our internal ledger.

process.title: Dispute
process.body: Incorrect charges are disputed and escalated until resolved.

tracking-notifications.detailedDescription: See every shipment across 11 clearance stages. From pre-arrival documents through vessel arrival, customs declaration, payment, inspection, port fees, release, loading, transit, and delivery. Clients get automatic updates on their channel of choice.
features: ETA updates on every stage change
advantages: No login required for client tracking
advantagesDescriptions: Your clients open a link. They never sign up or install anything.
Every template is drafted natively in both Arabic and English.
Every shipment has a unique tracking URL for your clients and partners.
Exceptions carry reason codes and responsible-party so disputes are clear.
Customers can export the full timeline as a PDF for their own records.

process.title: Tracking number assigned
process.body: A unique number is minted when the shipment is created.

process.title: Public link sent
process.body: The tracking link is shared with your client automatically.

process.title: Stage transitions
process.body: Operators advance the shipment through 11 defined stages.

process.title: Auto-notify
process.body: Each stage transition triggers the right notification channel.

process.title: ETA recalculation
process.body: We recompute ETA after every stage completion.

process.title: Delivery confirmation
process.body: Final POD locks the timeline and closes the shipment.

description.secondLine: and tools from vetted Port Sudan providers.

marketplace-logistics.detailedDescription: The Mazin marketplace connects you to vetted providers of trucks, forklifts, manpower crews, and tool rentals across Port Sudan. Transparent pricing, WhatsApp booking, and ratings you can trust.
features: Truck booking by tonnage and route
Manpower crews for loading and stuffing
advantages: Ratings and reviews from other clearers
advantagesDescriptions: Every provider is vetted for license, insurance, and track record.
Book in a couple of messages. No app install required.
Rates are published up front. No surprise charges at payment.
Community ratings flag reliable partners and weed out the weak ones.
Multiple provider invoices consolidated into one settlement.

process.title: Browse the catalog
process.body: Filter by category, area, and date available.

process.title: Open a service
process.body: Review rates, ratings, and provider profile.

process.title: Send inquiry
process.body: Submit your requirement via the inquiry form.

process.title: Provider responds
process.body: Providers respond via WhatsApp, phone, or email.

process.title: Confirm and pay
process.body: Agree terms and settle via the platform or direct.

process.title: Rate the experience
process.body: Post-service rating helps other clearers pick wisely.

description.firstLine: Mandatory ACD filing before vessel loading
description.secondLine: with ACN generation and B/L reconciliation.

advance-cargo-declaration.detailedDescription: ACD is mandatory from January 2026. File your Advance Cargo Declaration five days before vessel arrival, get your ACN number on the Bill of Lading, and avoid port rejection. We handle the entire lifecycle from filing to amendments.
features: ACD filing before cargo loading at origin
ACN number generation and B/L placement
advantagesDescriptions: Our workflow enforces the 5-day deadline so you never miss the window.
We submit electronically without manual data re-entry anywhere.
Every draft is scored for rejection risk before submission.
Amendments route through a guided workflow that prevents data drift.
Every change is logged with timestamp, actor, and reason.

process.title: Cargo data capture
process.body: We capture cargo details from the supplier before loading.

process.title: Compliance check
process.body: Data is validated against Sudan Customs rules.

process.title: ACD filing
process.body: We file the declaration and receive the ACN number.

process.title: B/L placement
process.body: ACN is placed on the Bill of Lading at origin.

process.title: Pre-arrival check
process.body: We reconcile manifest against ACD before vessel arrival.

process.title: Amendment
process.body: Any discrepancy is amended through the guided workflow.

description.firstLine: Bank IM Form preparation for foreign

im-form-processing.detailedDescription: The IM Form is required by Bank of Sudan for foreign currency allocation on imports. We prepare, submit, track, and close IM Forms for every shipment with LC or bank transfer payment terms.
advantages: Historical record of every IM reference
advantagesDescriptions: Draft IM Form ready within one working day of instruction.
We lodge directly with the bank approved for your foreign currency.
LC and IM Form numbers cross-referenced at every step.
Closure variances flagged automatically so you can investigate early.
Every historical IM reference is searchable in the dashboard.

process.title: Proforma receipt
process.body: The supplier proforma invoice triggers the IM workflow.

process.title: Form preparation
process.body: We draft the IM Form using verified supplier data.

process.title: Bank submission
process.body: We lodge the form with your chosen correspondent bank.

process.title: Allocation
process.body: Bank of Sudan allocates foreign currency against the form.

process.title: LC or transfer
process.body: Payment issues to supplier under the allocated IM.

process.title: Closure
process.body: Form is closed after shipment arrival and document settlement.
